---
title : "Figure 11.4"
subtitle : "Double descent for random linear projections"
author : "Thimoté Dupuch"
date : today
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    theme:
      light: zephyr
      dark: darkly
engine: julia
---

```{julia}
#| echo : false
#| output : false
using Pkg
Pkg.activate("../..")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
using LinearAlgebra, Random, Statistics, CairoMakie
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
#| echo : false
CairoMakie.set_theme!(theme_latexfonts())
CairoMakie.activate!(type = "svg")
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
blue, orange, green, pink, teal, red, yellow = Makie.wong_colors()
```

```{julia}
#| output : false
function run_random_projections(; seed=0, n=200, d=400, nrep=20, std_noise=0.5)
    rng = MersenneTwister(seed)

    # Covariance Matrix Sigma
    temp = randn(rng, d, d)
    temp = temp + temp'
    F = eigen(temp)
    Sigma = F.vectors * Diagonal(1.0 ./ (1:d)) * F.vectors'
    Sigma = Sigma / trace(Sigma)

    # Matrix square root
    F_sig = eigen(Sigma)
    Sigmasqrt = F_sig.vectors * Diagonal(sqrt.(max.(F_sig.values, 0.0))) * F_sig.vectors'

    theta_ast = randn(rng, d)
    theta_ast = theta_ast / sqrt(dot(theta_ast, Sigma * theta_ast))

    ms = collect(0:2:2*d)
    # Remove ms too close to n to avoid numerical instability
    filter!(m -> abs(m - n) >= 5, ms)

    empRvar = fill(Inf, length(ms), nrep)
    empRbias = fill(Inf, length(ms), nrep)

    for irep in 1:nrep
        X = sign.(randn(rng, n, d)) * Sigmasqrt
        epsilon = sign.(randn(rng, n))
        Sfull = sign.(randn(rng, d, 2 * d))

        for (im, m) in enumerate(ms)
            if m == 0
                thetabias = zeros(d)
                thetavar = zeros(d)

                empRvar[im, irep] = dot(thetavar, Sigma * thetavar)
                empRbias[im, irep] = dot(thetabias .- theta_ast, Sigma * (thetabias .- theta_ast))
            else
                S = Sfull[:, 1:m]

                if m < n
                    # Solve under-parameterized projection
                    XS = X * S
                    invSXXS = inv(XS' * XS)
                    thetabias = S * (invSXXS * (XS' * (X * theta_ast)))
                    thetavar = S * (invSXXS * (XS' * epsilon))
                else
                    # Solve over-parameterized projection
                    XS = X * S
                    invXSSX = inv(XS * XS')
                    thetabias = S * (XS' * (invXSSX * (X * theta_ast)))
                    thetavar = S * (XS' * (invXSSX * epsilon))
                end

                empRvar[im, irep] = dot(thetavar, Sigma * thetavar)
                empRbias[im, irep] = dot(thetabias .- theta_ast, Sigma * (thetabias .- theta_ast))
            end
        end
    end

    mean_bias = mean(empRbias, dims=2)[:, 1]
    mean_var = mean(empRvar, dims=2)[:, 1]
    std_total = std(empRbias + std_noise^2 * empRvar, dims=2)[:, 1]

    excess_risk = mean_bias + std_noise^2 * mean_var

    return ms, excess_risk, std_total
end
```

```{julia}
ms, excess_risk, std_total = run_random_projections()
```

```{julia}
fig = Figure(size=(800, 600), fontsize=18)
ax = Axis(fig[1, 1], xlabel="m", ylabel="excess risks", title="Double descent for random linear projections", limits=((0, maximum(ms)), (0, 4.0)))

lines!(ax, ms, excess_risk, color=blue, linewidth=2, label="mean")
lines!(ax, ms, excess_risk + std_total, color=blue, linestyle=:dash, linewidth=2, label="mean ± std")

temp_lower = excess_risk - std_total
# Set negative elements to a large value or ignore for visualization as in Matlab
temp_lower[temp_lower .< 0] .= NaN
lines!(ax, ms, temp_lower, color=blue, linestyle=:dash, linewidth=2)

axislegend(ax, position=:rt)

fig
```
